### The Piazza della Signoria in Florence The painting shows a topographically accurate view of the Piazza della Signoria looking east, with the Palazzo Vecchio and the Loggia dei Lanzi, and figures gathered around a puppet-show. | - Oil on canvas, 57 x 87 cm Private collection //Welcome to the Web Gallery of Art! Please enter here to visit the  ...    | The Piazza della Signoria takes its name from the Palazzo Vecchio (or Palazzo della Signoria), which from the 13th century was the meeting-place for government and is still used as the town hall of Florence today. Color: Uncoloured.  ...    Lively view of Nunziata square with the statue of Ferdinand I in Florence after Giuseppe Zocchi (1711or 1717 - 1767) and engraved by B.Sgrilli. From "Scelta di XXIV Vedute delle principali contrade, piazze, chiese, e palazzi della Città di Firenze".The 24 plates in this work show views in Florence. The scenes are enlivened by human activity.
